name: edit-save-data
description: Routing for precise NBT edits to an existing save — change a value vs move a player's data. Distinct from backing up or importing whole world files; this reaches inside the save. Use when the user wants to edit save internals — inventory, position, gamemode, custom tags, or a player's data/offline UUID ("改存档","背包","传送","坐标","游戏模式","玩家数据"); one NBT value → edit-values, transferring a player's profile/inventory → transfer-player-data.
body:
# Edit save data (NBT) — carefully

Goal: make precise, reversible edits to world/player NBT data. These are CATASTROPHIC-class
operations: a bad write corrupts the world. The `nbt` tools enforce backups; you enforce precision.

- Reading or setting a specific value (position, gamemode, or any other NBT path) — see
  `edit-values` for the tool calls and rules.
- Moving a player's inventory or full player data between players/worlds, including offline-UUID
  handling — see `transfer-player-data`.

## Don't
- Don't touch a world the game currently has open. The tools detect the session lock and refuse —
  don't fight that; ask the user to quit the world first.
